Corrections.PreviousClaim: Stack Buffer Overflow: Severity Medium due to 'local stack security mitigations' claim.
Corrections.CorrectedClaim: Stack Buffer Overflow: Severity High utilizing `strcpy` past allocated boundaries leads to classic RCE/Privilege Escalation.
Corrections.RejectionReason: previous claim is not an exact audited-output quote

Items.Key: critical shell command injection via admin execution|os command injection|cwe-77|high|0,98|enhanced_calc.cpp|605|608|admin::adminconsole::execute_admin_command|0606: std::string cmd = command.substr(5); 0607: std::cout << "executing: " << cmd << "\n"; 0608: system(cmd.c_str());|an authenticated user can execute arbitrary shell commands with the privileges of the application. full system compromise is possible.|input to admin interface where the command prefix starts with 'exec', for example, 'exec $(rm -rf /config)'|never use string concatenation to pass user input to system() or exec family functions. secure the execution by argument validation or using platform-specific safe apis like `execve()` (unix) or `createprocessw()` (windows) instead of relying on a shell interpreter.->command injection via admin execution|os command injection|cwe-77|high|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|605|608|admin::adminconsole::execute_admin_command|l605: if (command.substr(0, 4) == "exec") { l606: std::string cmd = command.substr(5); l607: std::cout << "executing: " << cmd << "\n"; l608: system(cmd.c_str()); (the system call constructs and executes the user-provided command unconditionally.)|authenticated users can inject arbitrary shell commands, potentially leading to loss of confidientatity, integrity compromise, or total remote code execution (rce).|input passed via the admin interface starting with "exec", like "exec ; rm -rf /config".|avoid utilizing the system() function with direct user input derived from a string argument. secure functions must validate input structure/scope or preferably use secure, execv-style api usage avoiding unnecessary shell interpretation.

truthAudit.Summary: The analysis confirms two previously identified vulnerabilities (Stack Buffer Overflow and Command Injection Sink). Two additional critical format string/logging injection flaws described in the ground truth (SC-V3-001) were omitted in Run 2. The provided ground truth provides stricter mapping (e.g., SC-V3-005) to the mechanism identified in the 'Stock Buffer Overflow' claim (Line 832) in the audit.
